notes: Replaced bootstrap placeholder with an actual Alpha-lane critic failure result.
Read current claim inventory before writing; active-claim registry returned zero live claims, and sampled recent noncanonical claim YAMLs T-9002 through T-9009 all show DONE status.

evidence.summary: C45 already compiles each request into a sovereign execution plan, selects execution mode, binds authority, and enforces provenance. An independent inference layer that chooses backend class, failover, or routing becomes a duplicate control plane.

C45 explicitly locks execution mode and forbids silent drift between native and leased operation. A shared inference plane pressures the system to reintroduce exactly that drift for efficiency and availability.

C42 says execution priming is stronger than warm-state reuse but still does not create a second runtime system. A persistent inference service with reusable sessions and queued work is the most likely place for priming to turn into shadow runtime authority.

C42 requires a C23 lease before active runtime work begins. Any independent inference layer that starts long-running work on the strength of cached context or preallocated backend state violates that boundary.

C36 is not a generic API gateway. If the new layer needs direct enterprise, foundry, or agent-facing access to justify its separation, it immediately collides with EMA-I's membrane-owned receptor model.

All external interactions must pass through EMA-I receptors. A separately managed inference layer creates recurring pressure for a bypass path because teams will want direct model-serving access instead of receptor-mediated bounded execution.

C39 already defines delegated sampling results with required model provenance. A new inference layer either duplicates this semantic contract or hides realized execution facts behind noncanonical internal RPC behavior.

C47 is explicit that C45 chooses whether a route uses leased or native execution and C47 supplies promoted native models. A separate layer blurs this split and turns native promotion into a plugin detail instead of the migration driver.
C47 forbids reintroducing runtime bridge dependency. A general inference microservice tier is the easiest place for permanent provider-shaped dependencies to hide under a new name.

C18 already treats sovereign compute, leased cognition, and Forge adaptation as explicit cost buckets. Reintroducing a separate inference layer does not remove these costs; it adds another platform surface that duplicates governance and operating expense.
C22 already assumes bounded leased-provider wrappers plus local/native fallback. The implementation gap is controlled substrate management, not revival of an independent service layer.

T-300 fixes Alternative C forward authority through C38, C39, C40, C41, C42, C45, C46, C47, and T-290. Reintroducing a historical-style inference microservice as new forward doctrine is boundary drift by definition.
The T-300 claim closeout records the same boundary in governance form: C4-era ideas remain retained only for historical, audit, retrofit, and migration use, not as forward authority.

lane_strategy: Improve the current task path without breaking core assumptions.

model_audit.routing_basis: User-directed real future-branch child execution for T-9013 in Codex.

objective: Explore the most plausible failure cascade future for the current ideation problem. Apply that branch specifically to 'Independent Model Inference Microservice Layer'.
one_sentence_thesis: The likeliest failure is a quiet authority split, not a dramatic crash: the reintroduced inference layer starts as an internal efficiency surface, then becomes a de facto second runtime and routing authority that weakens C45 locks, turns C42 priming into shadow execution, slows C47 native migration, and leaves Atrahasis with permanent non-sovereign model operations dressed up as infrastructure.
plausibility_rationale: This failure path is plausible because it begins with a reasonable local optimization: separate lightweight agents from heavyweight GPU serving without openly changing doctrine.
It does not require anyone to announce a doctrinal break. Each step can be justified as convenience, latency reduction, batching efficiency, or operational simplification.
That makes it more likely than an overt governance rupture: the system drifts into invalid authority shape incrementally while still sounding architecturally prudent.
failure_trigger: The idea is reintroduced as an independently operated shared model-serving plane, nominally internal-only, that handles backend placement, batching, warm-state reuse, and failover for many agents.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: Internal-only reinterpretation softens the boundary
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: To avoid obvious conflict with current doctrine, the layer is framed as an implementation convenience rather than a new public subsystem. In practice it still acquires its own scheduler, backend pool, session cache, and routing logic.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: C45 becomes a thin wrapper around decisions made elsewhere
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: Because the new layer controls which backend actually runs, C45 no longer meaningfully owns native-versus-leased execution choice, failover, or provider posture. Route authority is duplicated and eventually eroded.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: Warm-state reuse mutates into shadow runtime authority
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: The service starts holding reusable contexts, queued work, and long-lived backend sessions to save latency. Those operational shortcuts function like execution rights before or outside explicit C23 lease issuance, violating the C42 rule that priming is necessary but insufficient.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: Membrane and message discipline start drifting
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: Teams want direct enterprise or machine access to the faster shared service, or they add internal RPC semantics that bypass canonical sampling_result and model-provenance disclosure. C36 and C39 are then treated as outer wrappers instead of owning surfaces.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: C47 promotion incentives weaken
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: Once the shared layer is optimized around pooled leased backends, native promoted models are onboarded as just another backend option. The system stops treating native promotion as a path to retire leased dependence and instead treats leased dependence as the stable operating center.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: C18 and C22 absorb permanent hybrid-operating cost
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: The promised efficiency gains are offset by a new platform budget: duplicated observability, compliance, capacity controls, provenance plumbing, and provider governance. The implementation roadmap now has to carry an extra always-on service plane instead of a bounded internal seam.

most_plausible_failure_cascade.title: The layer survives only by becoming doctrinal debt
most_plausible_failure_cascade.detail: At that point Atrahasis either pays to unwind the layer back into C45/C47-aligned internals or keeps a permanent sovereignty exception whose architecture no longer matches T-300's forward-authority posture.

failure_outcome: Instead of achieving clean agent-model decoupling, Atrahasis ends up with duplicated runtime authority, weaker provenance discipline, slower native migration, and a durable leased-cognition dependency hidden behind an internal service abstraction.
recommended_next_action: Do not reintroduce the idea as an independent layer. If the objective still matters, salvage it only as a narrow internal capacity-control seam under C45 with explicit C42 handoff discipline, C47 promotion ownership, C39 provenance visibility, and C18/C22 cost governance.
